reasoning: The statistical trend model from FRED data suggests a slight positive drift in the 1-year expected inflation series over the past year (+0.0006 per day), resulting in an estimated 58% chance of an increase in the near term. The series exhibits high month-to-month volatility with a standard deviation of differences of roughly 0.316 compared to a very small positive monthly drift. Historical data shows that while the long-term trend has a slight upward tilt, the last few months have experienced downward momentum (3 of the last 4 months had drops, from 2.80 to 2.70). Because the long-term deterministic drift is small compared to the noise (diff variance), the random walk dominates and the probability is around 53-58%. Given the instruction to move distant dates closer to 0.5, I assign probabilities slightly above 0.50 that the value will have increased, decreasing slightly as the horizon increases.
